records.ety: [L. sedentarius, fr. sedere to sit: cf. F. sedentaire. See Sedent.]

defs: Accustomed to sit much or long; as, a sedentary man.
Characterized by, or requiring, much sitting; as, a sedentary employment; a sedentary life.
quotes: Any education that confined itself to sedentary pursuits was essentially imperfect.

defs: Inactive; motionless; sluggish; hence, calm; tranquil.
quotes: The soul, considered abstractly from its passions, is of a remiss, sedentary nature.

defs: Remaining in one place, especially when firmly attached to some object; as, the oyster is a sedentary mollusk; the barnacles are sedentary crustaceans.
